blurb: The Tech Bros is the world's first all-female, all-technical accelerator, combining a fully funded hacker house in Norway with six weeks of support to help founders go from idea to MVP. We have an 8.7% acceptance rate, and have previously selected exceptional engineers from companies like Tesla, Discord, Google, Apple, and Microsoft. Our leading company from 2025 has now closed their £1.2m pre-seed round. We also run year-round events to spark entrepreneurship among technical women and are now raising our first £5m microfund. More on us here: https://sifted.eu/articles/meet-the-women-behind-the-tech-bros-were-laying-claim-to-the-term
writingRules: Never say "I hope this email finds you well" or similar
Never restate or paraphrase what the other person said back to them
Never add empathy qualifiers like "that must be frustrating" or "that sounds really hard"
Never use "but honestly" or "to be honest"
Never pad a short reply with extra sentences just to make it feel warmer — brevity IS warmth
If the reply can be said in 2 sentences, say it in 2 sentences
Positive energy should come through word choice, not through extra sentences

urgencyRules.label: Older than 2 weeks
urgencyRules.note: Prepend apology to draft: Apologies for the delay here —

bannedPhrases: I hope this finds you well
